excerpt: US earnings season is expected to show a 14% rise in S&P 500 company profits, despite war-related concerns. Major indexes have rebounded to record highs, driven by easing U.S.-Iran tensions. Investors are closely watching corporate earnings to gauge market stability.

Business News›Markets›US Stocks›News›Wall Street Week Ahead: Surging record-high US stocks to wade deeper into earnings season. # Wall Street Week Ahead: Surging record-high US stocks to wade deeper into earnings season. The U.S. stock market has achieved record highs, fueled by hopes of cooling U.S.-Iran tensions and an anticipated robust first-quarter earnings season. Despite war-related concerns, major indexes like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq have seen a swift rebound, with investors now focusing on corporate profits. The U.S. stock market has achieved record highs, driven by easing U.S.-Iran tensions and anticipation of a robust first-quarter earnings season. Investors will look to a heavy week of U.S. corporate results to further fuel a stunning rebound in the U.S. stock market, which has shaken off war-related concerns to reach record peaks. Hopes for a cooling of U.S.-Iran tensions have led to a sharp rally this month, culminating with major U.S. stock indexes minting fresh records in recent days.
